rationale: SoxXA is a known sulfur-oxidation cytochrome module and should serve as a positive interface control.
SoxYZ is a cognate carrier module and should show strong same-route interface support.
Tests whether the carrier module and SoxB form a plausible functional route assembly.
Tests whether SoxXA and SoxYZ remain structurally compatible in a route-localized sulfur oxidation module.
SoxCD is a route-module candidate when both subunits are available.
FccAB is a cognate lineage-core sulfur-energy complex and should pass a positive interface gate.
SoeABC is a backbone sulfur oxidation complex; membrane subunit may lower confidence and should be interpreted cautiously.
HdrABC-like complex tests lineage-core/backbone route compatibility.
DsrAB is an optional cognate complex if high-quality paired sequences are available.
Tests whether a mobile-boundary protein forms a nonspecific interface with a SOX catalytic protein.
Tests mobile-boundary specificity against a SOX carrier protein.
Tests mobile-boundary specificity against a lineage-core backbone protein.
Tests whether backbone route proteins create nonspecific interfaces with mobile markers.
Tests membrane/backbone route specificity against a mobile marker.
Optional same-genome housekeeping decoy if sequence is available.
Optional same-genome housekeeping decoy if sequence is available.
